message: 🌱 Add caddy: Metrics monitoring in v2 mission (#466)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "caddy-3390-metrics-monitoring-in-v2",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "caddy: Metrics monitoring in v2",
+    "description": "Metrics monitoring in v2. This issue affects 14+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify caddy feature symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your caddy deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=caddy\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors related to: Metrics monitoring in v2"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Check current resource configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant caddy resources:\n```bash\nkubectl get resource -A\nkubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>\n```\nI've been using the Prometheus support in Caddy 1.x. This has worked extremely well. I would like to be able to upgrade to v2, but it doesn't seem like there's any out of the box monitoring options.\n\n"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Metrics monitoring in v2",
+        "description": "@SuperQ\n\n> Do you want to take a first pass\n\nSure - I can try and fit this in over the next couple days.\n\n> If the metrics handler doubles as a Caddy HTTP handler module like I described, then the listening port and path are all taken care of by the user's config, no need to separately configure tha\n```yaml\n{\r\n  \"apps\": {\r\n    \"http\": {\r\n      \"servers\": {\r\n        \"srv0\": {\r\n          \"listen\": [ \":9180\" ],\r\n          \"routes\": {\r\n            \"match\": [{ \"path\": [ \"/metrics\" ] }],\r\n            \"handle\": [{ \"handler\": \"prometheus\" }],\r\n            \"terminal\": true\r\n          }\r\n        }\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n}\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Metrics monitoring in v2 is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The root cause is: @SuperQ\n\n> Do you want to take a first pass\n\nSure - I can try and fit this in over the next couple days.\n\n> If the metrics handler doubles as a Caddy HTTP handler module like I described, then the listening port and path are all taken care of by the user's config, no need to separately configure that in the metrics module.\n\n@mholt If I'm interpreting this right, I think what you're envisioning is . This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "{\r\n  \"apps\": {\r\n    \"http\": {\r\n      \"servers\": {\r\n        \"srv0\": {\r\n          \"listen\": [ \":9180\" ],\r\n          \"routes\": {\r\n            \"match\": [{ \"path\": [ \"/metrics\" ] }],\r\n            \"handle\": [{ \"handler\": \"prometheus\" }],\r\n            \"terminal\": true\r\n          }\r\n        }\r\n      }\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n}",
+        ":9180/metrics\r\n\r\nprometheus"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "caddy",
+      "community",
+      "web-server",
+      "feature"
+    ],

+    },

+  },
